attrs.content: What is the sign for "hair" in American Sign Language (ASL)?
attrs.name: description

text_preview: "hair" American Sign Language (ASL) ASL Lessons | Bookstore | Library | ASL University Main ► HAIR: The American Sign Language (ASL) sign for "hair" The concept of "hair" is often understood from context and thus doesn't need a separate sign. If you are describing someone and you do the sign "STRAIGHT HAIR" there is no need to also do the sign HAIR. The general sign for "hair" starts as an open-"F"-hand held near the head and then changes into a regular "F"-hand as the flat of the thumbnail comes near and/or makes contact with the head. HAIR: HAIR (g-handshape version): The sign HAIR uses a "close, open, close" movement, but during high speed signing of sentences it is common to use just a single movement. Especially in compound signs like "brunette." CURLY-hair: Or to show LONG CURLY-HAIR
